$schema_note: Output-type presets for the Podcast Production Engine (Skill 58). A preset is a named bundle of production mode, pipeline scope, and deliverable set, resolved at Step 1 (Ingest) and persisted by podcast_state.py (the sole ledger writer). The preset field lives in the canonical intake payload AND in the ledger. Presets INTERVIEW and SOLO derive their default from the Production Mode; SEASON_STRATEGY and EPISODE_ASSET_PACK are thin variations over the same pipeline and state machine, explicitly requested rather than survey-derived. Writing rules for everything this skill produces: zero em dash characters, no triple backtick or code-fence markers. Runtime content work routes to the client's own models only (Ollama Cloud Kimi 2.6, then GLM 5.2, then the OpenRouter equivalents, then Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite); no build-time or non-client model, provider, package, key, or host is present in this file or any runtime file.

order: If the intake payload carries an explicit preset value in the enum, use it.
Else derive the default from the resolved Production Mode via mode_default_preset.
season_strategy and episode_asset_pack have no mode default; they must be explicitly requested (operator-initiated) and their referenced source record, when required, must exist in the ledger.
resolution.written_by: podcast_state.py (sole writer); the preset is recorded on the ledger record and echoed in the delivery report.

shared_state_note: All presets reuse the ONE shared ledger state enum (received, researching, writing, qc, art, audio, publishing, enrolling, complete, plus cost_hold, credit_out, failed) owned by podcast_state.py. INTERVIEW and SOLO traverse the full path to complete; SEASON_STRATEGY and EPISODE_ASSET_PACK traverse a subset of it and still end at complete. No preset defines new states.
qc_gate_note: The Episode Gate (Gate B: 16 Tier 1 hard-fail checks plus the 10-dimension rubric at 8 or higher, with the 3-strike cap) and the build/merge gate (Gate A: the 10-category fleet rubric at 8.5) are never conflated or substituted. INTERVIEW and SOLO produce an episode and are decided by the Episode Gate. SEASON_STRATEGY produces a document, not an episode, so it is judged by a document-quality review (fabrication check plus rendering and formatting standards); this is neither the Episode Gate nor the build/merge decision gate, and no Tier 1 episode check or episode rubric is applied because no episode exists. EPISODE_ASSET_PACK does not re-write the already-passed script, so it applies only the mechanical asset checks (cover ffmpeg and ffprobe assertions, document rendering and font floors, teaser checks) to the regenerated assets.

interview.description: Full 18-step run for the SHUA lead-generation path: the host presents an everyday guest's ideas, the guest feels Seen, Heard, Understood, and Acknowledged, and the book teaser is the coup de grace.
interview.workflow_note: Enroll only after the episode is published to Podbean and the link-back field writes complete. Workflow names and trigger mechanism are discovered per client at setup via Skill 44, never guessed; 04-Podcast is Completed is field-triggered by contact.podcast_survey_episode_url changing, so verify whether it already fired and guard against double enrollment. Enrollment confirmed with caf verification reads.
interview.book_teaser_note: Written at Step 13 on Ollama Cloud Kimi 2.6 or GLM 5.2 (thinking high, then the same routing fallbacks); rendered as a book-typeset PDF; link written to contact.book_teaser when the field exists, else surfaced as a founder reminder and never silently created.
interview.source_doctrine: Companion document Interview Style mode; Skill 23 role-library podcast doctrine (director-of-podcast, podcast-host, audio-post-producer, qc-specialist-podcast).

solo.description: Full episode run minus the book teaser and minus workflow enrollment; the client's own weekly episode in their own cloned voice. The running episode spreadsheet is updated instead of any customer messaging.
solo.hard_refusal_note: Personal Podcast Style HARD-REFUSES the two customer-notification workflows (they are the Interview lead-generation path only). At Step 17 the enrollment layer no-ops the workflows and appends the episode row to the running spreadsheet created once at onboarding. Zero SMS and zero email are ever sent on this path; Convert and Flow owns all messaging and there is nothing to send here.
solo.scheduling_note: If contact.date_for_release is present and in the future, use it as the Podbean publish timestamp so the episode is scheduled rather than published immediately.
solo.source_doctrine: Companion document Personal Podcast Style mode.

season_strategy.description: A planning deliverable, not an episode: a season arc, an episode slate, and the style and mode per slot, drawing on the Skill 23 director-of-podcast doctrine. No render and no publish; produces an Episode Package-style strategy document only.

adapted_steps.4: size becomes slate sizing: how many episodes the season holds.
adapted_steps.5: blueprint becomes the season arc and episode slate, assigning a style and a mode to each slot.
adapted_steps.strategy_document: a preset-specific render replaces the standard documents step (12): the Episode Package-style strategy document is produced and linked, with no episode media.

season_strategy.step0_note: Step 0 for this preset validates only the credentials the preset actually uses (the web research tool and the document tooling); the render, publish, and enrollment credentials are not required because none of those stages run.
season_strategy.mode_module: modes/interview.md or modes/personal.md per the style and mode assigned to each slate slot
season_strategy.source_doctrine: Skill 23 role-library director-of-podcast season-planning doctrine; podcast-producer supports.

episode_asset_pack.description: Regenerates or completes the asset set (cover, documents, teaser) for an EXISTING episode without re-writing or re-publishing the audio. Idempotent against the ledger. Useful for repairs and for Skill 57 social-packaging handoffs (the sanctioned downstream read).

step_conditions.10: regenerate cover art only when requested.
step_conditions.12: regenerate or complete the Episode Package and Speech Script documents.
step_conditions.13: book teaser only when the source episode is Interview Style and the teaser is requested or missing.
step_conditions.14: upload the regenerated assets to the media library.
step_conditions.16: write only the regenerated asset links; contact.podcast_survey_episode_url is NOT re-written unless it is missing, to avoid re-triggering field-driven workflows.

episode_asset_pack.hard_refusal_note: Audio is never re-rendered and the episode is never re-published (the idempotent permalink guard skips Step 15 when a permalink already exists in the ledger). The already-passed script is never re-written; the frozen research package from the source record is reused, never re-run.
episode_asset_pack.source_reference_note: Requires a valid existing episode record in the ledger; the mode and style are inherited from that record. Missing or ambiguous source record stops the run with an operator surface, never a guess.
episode_asset_pack.mode_module: inherited from the source episode record
episode_asset_pack.source_doctrine: Companion document asset regeneration and repair posture; Skill 57 social-packaging handoff boundary.
